name: Liu Xiao (Zhongning)
page_title: Liu Xiao (Zhongning) 劉囂 (重寧) [Han, Dong Zhuo] – Romance of the Three Kingdoms Encyclopedia – Kongming’s Archives

sections.short-biography: Gained rank due to closeness with the eunuchs, inspired fear in the people.

	 Wade-Giles 
	Liu Hsiao (Ch‘ung-ning)

	 Birthplace 
	Changsha commandery

	 Other Names 
	Liu Ao (alternative romanization); Liu Xiao/Liu Ao (劉嚻)

	 Name Notes 
	Liu Xiao’s personal name, 囂, may be seen Romanized as Xiao or An in various sources with deference to the translator’s interpretation.

sections.biography-source-undefined:
Liu Xiao, styled Zhongning, was from Changsha commandery. He served as Minister Steward of Changle palace (長樂少府),[HHS113] then Minister Coachman before becoming Excellency of Works in the sixth lunar month of 169.[HHS9] It is said he was promoted to Excellency of Works because of his friendly connections to eunuchs.[SGZ6] He resigned in the seventh month of 170.[HHS9]

He would disappear from historical records until about 192, when Dong Zhuo tasked him with hunting down sons that were not filial, treacherous ministers, corrupt officials, and disobedient younger brothers. Liu Xiao was Colonel who Directs Retainers at that time. Those that were hunted down were executed and their properties seized by the state. It is said that many of the killed people were innocent, but whether this was due to Liu Xiao is not clarified upon.[SGZ6]

full_text:
Liu Xiao (Zhongning) 劉囂 (重寧)
ARomance of the Three KingdomsEncyclopedia officer profile.
Liu Xiao (劉囂), styledZhongning (重寧), was born in Changsha commandery. Gained rank due to closeness with the eunuchs, inspired fear in the people. Liu Xiao was affiliated with the Han Dynasty and Dong Zhuo.

BiographySource Undefined
Liu Xiao, styled Zhongning, was from Changsha commandery. He served as Minister Steward of Changle palace (長樂少府),[HHS113] then Minister Coachman before becoming Excellency of Works in the sixth lunar month of 169.[HHS9] It is said he was promoted to Excellency of Works because of his friendly connections to eunuchs.[SGZ6] He resigned in the seventh month of 170.[HHS9]
He would disappear from historical records until about 192, when Dong Zhuo tasked him with hunting down sons that were not filial, treacherous ministers, corrupt officials, and disobedient younger brothers. Liu Xiao was Colonel who Directs Retainers at that time. Those that were hunted down were executed and their properties seized by the state. It is said that many of the killed people were innocent, but whether this was due to Liu Xiao is not clarified upon.[SGZ6]
